publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-01-29 18:06:58 -0500.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Collection includes a series of letters from Charles William Wall; other Civil War letters from William Emerson, Henry Harrington, and Charles McCarthy; letters from Jane Middleton; and an autobiographical sketch of Andrew Wall.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Collection includes a series of letters from Charles William Wall, a sergeant in the 151st Regiment, New York Volunteers, mainly concerning troop movements, camp experiences, and other aspects of his life as a Union Army soldier in Maryland, Virginia, Canada, and Washington, D.C., but also dealing with his employment by a mining and milling company in Colorado (1869); other Civil War letters from William Emerson, Henry Harrington, and Charles McCarthy; letters from Jane Middleton, telling of her work in New York City at the Christian Female Home with indigent women (1864); and an autobiographical sketch (1881) of Andrew Wall, who had emigrated from England in 1833.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: Related collection: Aram Houssig Dimijian. World War I Letters, #2014.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Wall Family. Papers, #2012. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Women, Poor -- New York (State) -- New York.
United States -- History -- Civil War, 1861-1865.
Mills and mill-work.
Camps (Military)
controlaccess.corpname: United States. Army. Regiment Infantry, New York Volunteers, 151st

unittitle.extref: Link to a partial finding aid.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: All the material in the collection may not be covered by this guide.

did.unittitle: William Emerson to Mrs. Andrew Wall

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One letter only from William Emerson, a family friend, to Mr. Andrew Wall from La Fayette Barracks, Baltimore, Md., Head Quarters 151st. N.Y.V. The Emerson family were living at or near the same camp where Charles Wall was stationed at this time.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Henry (Hank) Harrington, Charles's cousin, wrote to Ellen Wall, Charles's sister while he was away at war. From Elmira Rendezvous Barracks No.5 Co. K; Camp Anderson Regt. 27 Co. K; Camp Vernon, Va., near Richmond, Va., New York City

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 2 letters - Private Charles McCarthy was stationed at Fort Monroe, Va. He enlisted in Rochester, N.Y. on Aug. 6, 1860 in Co. B 2nd Regt. of Artillery.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 8 letters from Jane Middleton to the Wall family (7 to Mr. Wall, 1 to Mrs. Wall). 1 rent receipt to a Mrs. Smith. The Home for the indigent aged respectable Females at 20th St. in a letter dated Jan. 20, 1864. "Christian Female Home" is mentioned in a letter dated Feb. 27, 1864

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Charles Wall was in Colorado working for a mining company in 1869-1870 His cousin, Henry (Hank) Harrington was with him at this time. Charles W. Wall's letters go until 1870.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Joseph Wall wrote 3 letters to his father, Andrew Wall. In 1860 Joseph Wall (not Charles) went to Canada on business (Simon & Petterson) , he sold a piece of land in Canada to a Mr. Claffy. Joseph had to have his father and mother sign the deed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: There are also a number of letters to the Wall family from various relatives (Mostly cousins).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: additional items include: list of clothing and equipment, presumably government issue, no year. Confederate $20 bill. U.S. Internal Revenue Stamp for ten lbs. of tobacco ($3.20) (in fragments).
